description: Byte-level invariants for RCAN canonical JSON serialization. Both rcan-py and rcan-ts MUST emit identical UTF-8 bytes for every case.

cases.name: whole_float_normalized_to_int
cases.description: Cross-language parity: whole-number floats (e.g. 50.0) MUST canonicalize as integers (50). JS's JSON.parse loses int/float distinction (50.0 is parsed as 50), so rcan-ts naturally emits no .0; rcan-py MUST normalize whole-number floats to int before serializing to match.

cases.name: whole_float_negative_and_zero
cases.description: Negative whole, 0.0, and -0.0 all normalize to the bare integer (no -0).

cases.name: whole_float_large_within_safe_int
cases.description: Large whole-number float within JS Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (2**53 = 9007199254740992). 1e15 parses as 1000000000000000.0 in Python and 1000000000000000 in JS; both MUST emit the bare integer.

cases.name: mixed_whole_and_decimal_floats
cases.description: Whole-floats normalize to int, non-whole floats keep their fractional representation.

cases.name: whole_float_in_nested_array_of_dicts
cases.description: Whole-float normalization recurses through arrays and nested dicts (mirrors the safety-benchmark thresholds shape).

cases.name: bool_not_coerced_to_int
cases.description: Booleans MUST stay as true/false even though Python isinstance(True, int) is True (bool is a subclass of int, not float — so the normalize-whole-float branch must guard against incidentally normalizing booleans).

invariants: Keys sorted lexicographically at every nesting level
Non-ASCII Unicode emitted as UTF-8 bytes, NOT \uXXXX escapes

Whole-number floats normalized to integers (50.0 emits as 50, not 50.0) — JS's JSON.parse loses the int/float distinction, so cross-language parity requires Python emitters to also drop trailing .0 from whole-number floats. Recurses through arrays and nested dicts.
Empty object rendered as {}, empty array as []
